ideation: This sequence simulates the act of riding a motorcycle.  The animation will focus on the upper body, emulating the hand and arm positions a rider might use to grip the handlebars.  Since the robot doesn't have hands, we'll approximate the grip. The left arm will mimic the clutch/brake, and the right will be positioned like the throttle.  We'll use subtle changes to make it look natural. Frame 1 will start with the arms slightly extended forward, simulating a relaxed posture before starting the bike. Frame 2 will show the arms bending slightly at the shoulder, with the left arm further inward to mimic a clutch/brake movement. Frame 3 will show a slight adjustment, with the left arm slightly straightened and the right arm pulled back. Frame 4 will show the arms returning to a more neutral position, but slightly forward to simulate the ongoing movement of riding.

frames.beat: arms extend forward, ready to ride
left arm pulls in for clutch, right arm eases back
adjusting grip, a subtle shift on the throttle
arms settle into a steady riding posture
